intro: Diplocaulobium validicolle is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of the South Pacific, particularly in Fiji and Vanuatu. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Diplocaulobium validicolle include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
